title: env: set "container" to current binary
body:
Instead of hard-coding "podman" as the "container" environment variable
set it to the current binary.  This way the package isn't tied to podman
and can be used by others (e.g., cri-o).

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

@rhatdan PTAL

If container is unset, libpod will auto-set it to "libpod" - should we not
set it at all in pkg/env and instead just do it somewhere in cmd/podman?

I would like to move this code out of libpod at some point, and share it with Buildah and CRI-o perhaps,

> I would like to move this code out of libpod at some point, and share it with Buildah and CRI-o perhaps,

This will happen once we separate podman and libpod. I am a bit scared of moving too much code to c/common at the moment as we only really test in libpod's CI.

Resending as GitHub seems to have lost my reply...

Strongly disagree with ctr=unknown - we do know, from the back end used to
create it. If we cannot sanely determine the binary name, we shouldn't set
anything so defaults will be used in Libpod.

Also, concern: does podman-remote now set container=podman-remote? Is it
bad if that is different between local and remote?
